cells.source:
# Smart Light Controller using OOPS

class SmartLight:
    """
    A class to represent a Smart Light.
    It can be turned ON or OFF and display its current status.
    """

    # Constructor to initialize light name and default status
    def __init__(self, light_name):
        self.light_name = light_name
        self.status = "OFF"   # Default state is OFF

    # Method to turn the light ON
    def turn_on(self):
        self.status = "ON"
        print(f"{self.light_name} is ON")

    # Method to turn the light OFF
    def turn_off(self):
        self.status = "OFF"
        print(f"{self.light_name} is OFF")

    # Method to display current status
    def display_status(self):
        print(f"{self.light_name} current status: {self.status}")


# ----------- Sample Usage -----------

# Taking input from user
light_name = input("Enter Light Name: ")
action = input("Enter Action (ON/OFF): ").upper()

# Creating object
light = SmartLight(light_name)

# Performing action
if action == "ON":
    light.turn_on()
elif action == "OFF":
    light.turn_off()
else:
    print("Invalid Action! Please enter ON or OFF.")

outputs.name: stdin
outputs.text:
Enter Light Name:  ABC
Enter Action (ON/OFF):  ON

cells.source:
# Employee ID Card System using OOPS

class Employee:
    """
    A class to store and display Employee ID Card details.
    """

    # Constructor to initialize employee details
    def __init__(self, name, emp_id, department):
        self.name = name
        self.emp_id = emp_id
        self.department = department

    # Method to display ID card details
    def display_id_card(self):
        print("\nEmployee ID Card")
        print("------------------------")
        print(f"Name: {self.name}")
        print(f"ID: {self.emp_id}")
        print(f"Department: {self.department}")


# ----------- Sample Usage -----------

# Taking user input
name = input("Enter Employee Name: ")
emp_id = input("Enter Employee ID: ")
department = input("Enter Department: ")

# Creating Employee object
employee = Employee(name, emp_id, department)

# Displaying ID Card
employee.display_id_card()

outputs.name: stdin
outputs.text:
Enter Employee Name:  ABC
Enter Employee ID:  123
Enter Department:  Class

outputs.name: stdout
outputs.text:
Employee ID Card
------------------------
Name: ABC
ID: 123
Department: Class

cells.source:
# Mobile Contact Record using OOPS

class Contact:
    """
    A class to store and display contact details.
    """

    # Constructor to initialize contact details
    def __init__(self, name, phone):
        self.name = name
        self.phone = phone

    # Method to display contact information
    def display_contact(self):
        print("\nContact Saved")
        print("--------------------")
        print(f"Name: {self.name}")
        print(f"Phone: {self.phone}")


# ----------- Sample Usage -----------

# Taking user input
name = input("Enter Contact Name: ")
phone = input("Enter Phone Number: ")

# Creating Contact object
contact = Contact(name, phone)

# Displaying Contact Information
contact.display_contact()

outputs.name: stdin
outputs.text:
Enter Contact Name:  Prajwal
Enter Phone Number:  567890

cells.source:
# Product Price Tag Generator using OOPS

class Product:
    """
    A class to store product details and generate a formatted price tag.
    """

    # Constructor to initialize product details
    def __init__(self, product_name, price):
        self.product_name = product_name
        self.price = price

    # Method to print formatted price tag
    def print_price_tag(self):
        print("\nProduct:", self.product_name)
        print(f"Price: ₹{self.price}")


# ----------- Sample Usage -----------

# Taking user input
product_name = input("Enter Product Name: ")
price = input("Enter Price: ")

# Creating Product object
product = Product(product_name, price)

# Printing price tag
product.print_price_tag()

outputs.name: stdin
outputs.text:
Enter Product Name:  Prajwal
Enter Price:  135

cells.source:
# Movie Rating Display System using OOPS

class Movie:
    """
    A class to store and display movie details.
    """

    # Constructor to initialize movie details
    def __init__(self, movie_name, rating):
        self.movie_name = movie_name
        self.rating = float(rating)  # Convert rating to float

    # Method to display movie details
    def display_movie(self):
        print("\nMovie:", self.movie_name)
        print(f"Rating: {self.rating} / 5")


# ----------- Sample Usage -----------

# Taking user input
movie_name = input("Enter Movie Name: ")
rating = input("Enter Rating: ")

# Creating Movie object
movie = Movie(movie_name, rating)

# Displaying Movie Details
movie.display_movie()

outputs.name: stdin
outputs.text:
Enter Movie Name:  ABC
Enter Rating:  10.0

cells.source:
# Delivery Address Manager using OOPS

class Delivery:
    """
    A class to store and display delivery details.
    """

    # Constructor to initialize customer details
    def __init__(self, customer_name, address):
        self.customer_name = customer_name
        self.address = address

    # Method to print delivery details
    def print_delivery_details(self):
        print("\nDelivery Details")
        print("------------------------")
        print(f"Customer: {self.customer_name}")
        print(f"Address: {self.address}")


# ----------- Sample Usage -----------

# Taking user input
customer_name = input("Enter Customer Name: ")
address = input("Enter Address: ")

# Creating Delivery object
delivery = Delivery(customer_name, address)

# Printing delivery details
delivery.print_delivery_details()

outputs.name: stdin
outputs.text:
Enter Customer Name:  Prajwal
Enter Address:  Namdhihalli

outputs.name: stdout
outputs.text:
Delivery Details
------------------------
Customer: Prajwal
Address: Namdhihalli
